description: Group plugins by readiness state in the side panel. Ready plugins appear at the top, not-ready plugins appear below a 'NOT CONNECTED' label at reduced opacity. Transitions between groups are smoothly animated.

userStories.title: Split PluginList into ready and not-ready groups with section label
userStories.description: As a user, I want ready plugins to appear at the top and not-ready plugins grouped below a 'NOT CONNECTED' label, so I can immediately see which plugins are usable without parsing individual icon states.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginList splits plugins into two groups: ready (tabState === 'ready') and not-ready (tabState === 'unavailable' or 'closed')
Ready plugins render first in their own Accordion with no label above them
Not-ready plugins render below in a separate Accordion with a 'NOT CONNECTED' label/header above them
The 'NOT CONNECTED' label uses: text-[10px] font-mono text-muted-foreground tracking-widest uppercase px-3 pt-3 pb-1
Not-ready plugin cards render at opacity-70 via a className on the Accordion.Item
The not-ready section (label + accordion) is hidden entirely when all plugins are ready — no empty label shown
The ready section has no label — ready is the default expected state
Failed plugins still render above everything as they do today (unchanged)
Both Accordion elements use type='multiple' so any card can be independently opened/closed
When a tool filter is active (toolFilter is non-empty), grouping is disabled — all matching plugins render in a single flat list as before (don't show 'NOT CONNECTED' label when filtering)
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes

userStories.title: Animate plugin cards moving between ready and not-ready groups
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see smooth animated transitions when a plugin becomes ready or not-ready, so the card movement between groups feels polished and intentional rather than jarring.
acceptanceCriteria: When a plugin transitions from not-ready to ready (e.g., user opens a Slack tab and logs in), its card smoothly animates from the not-ready group to the ready group
When a plugin transitions from ready to not-ready (e.g., user closes the tab), its card smoothly animates from the ready group to the not-ready group
The animation includes a fade-out at the old position, then a fade-in + slide-down at the new position, creating a natural relocation effect
The 'NOT CONNECTED' label appears/disappears smoothly (fade in/out) when the not-ready group gains or loses its first/last member
Cards that are not transitioning remain visually stable — they do not jump or flash during another card's transition
The animation duration is short enough to feel responsive (200-300ms total) but long enough to be perceivable
There is no layout shift or flickering during the transition
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
This is the most challenging story — smooth list reordering without a heavy animation library.

Recommended approach: use CSS transitions with a `key`-based tracking pattern.

1. **Track previous tabState per plugin** with a `useRef<Map<string, TabState>>()` in PluginList. On each render, compare current tabState with previous. If a plugin changed groups, mark it as "transitioning."

2. **Transition sequence** for a plugin moving from not-ready → ready:
   - Frame 1: Card is still in the not-ready group but starts fading out (`opacity-0 transition-opacity duration-200`)
   - After 200ms (via setTimeout or transitionend): Move the card to the ready group DOM, render it with `opacity-0 translate-y-2`
   - Next frame (requestAnimationFrame): Remove the translate/opacity classes so it fades in and slides up (`opacity-100 translate-y-0 transition-all duration-200`)
   - After another 200ms: Clear the transitioning state

3. **Simpler alternative** if the above is too complex: Skip the cross-group animation. Instead, use only fade effects:
   - Card fades out from old group (`opacity-0` over 150ms)
   - Card appears in new group with a fade-in (`opacity-0` → `opacity-100` over 200ms, or `opacity-70` for not-ready)
   - This is simpler to implement and still looks polished

4. **For the 'NOT CONNECTED' label**: wrap it in a div with `transition-all duration-200` and toggle between `opacity-100` / `opacity-0 h-0 overflow-hidden` based on whether notReadyPlugins.length > 0.

IMPORTANT: Do NOT install any animation libraries (framer-motion, react-spring, etc.). Use only CSS transitions + minimal React state. The retro theme is about simplicity.

Files to edit:
- `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginList.tsx` — Add transition state tracking and conditional CSS classes
- `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/styles.css` — Add any needed keyframe animations (though CSS transitions may suffice)

userStories.title: Update PluginList stories and verify E2E
userStories.description: As a developer, I want PluginList stories to demonstrate the ready/not-ready grouping and the full test suite to pass.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginList.stories.tsx has a story showing plugins split into ready and not-ready groups
PluginList.stories.tsx has a story showing all plugins ready (no 'NOT CONNECTED' section)
PluginList.stories.tsx has a story showing all plugins not-ready (no ready section, just the 'NOT CONNECTED' label + cards)
PluginList.stories.tsx has a story with a mix of ready, unavailable, and closed plugins
The existing Default, WithFailedPlugins, FilteredByTool, and NoFilterMatch stories are updated to work with the new grouping behavior
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
npm run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
File: `platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginList.stories.tsx`

The existing stories at lines 34-74 already have plugins with different tabStates (ready, closed, unavailable). The Default story should naturally show the grouping. Add these new stories:

1. **AllReady** — All plugins have `tabState: 'ready'`. No 'NOT CONNECTED' section should appear.
2. **AllNotReady** — All plugins have `tabState: 'closed'` or `tabState: 'unavailable'`. No ready section, just the label + faded cards.
3. **MixedReadiness** — 2 ready, 1 unavailable, 1 closed. Shows both groups clearly.
4. Update the existing **Default** story's mock data to ensure at least one plugin is ready and one is not-ready, so the grouping is visible by default.

The existing `FilteredByTool` story should show that grouping is disabled when filtering — all matching plugins in a single flat list.

IMPORTANT: E2E tests in `e2e/` may check for plugin card ordering or presence. Run `npm run test:e2e` to verify no E2E tests break. If they do, fix them — the side panel DOM structure changed (two Accordion elements instead of one).
